linearization: param(theta) = nominal + max(theta,0)*(Up - nominal) - max(-theta,0)*(nominal - Down)
comment: NewVarsCT18ZLambda6 + LatticeNoConstraintsFranksLambda4Neg NP model. tanh_6 on both sides + CT18ZNNLO PDF. Asymmetric Lambda_4 nuisance pairs lambda41.0 (Up) with the explicit lambda4-0.4 SCETlib template (Down), reaching further negative than the franks-vals (lambda40.0 Down) variant. CS-side gamma NP identical to LatticeNoConstraintsFranks. 2026-05-26.

important_conventions.TMD_Lambda4_asymmetric_to_negative: Down = lambda4-0.4 (explicit SCETlib template, not a linear extrapolation). Up = lambda41.0. Nominal = 0.4. d_up = 0.6, d_dn = 0.8.
important_conventions.tanh_6_NP_model: Both sides use np_model = tanh_6 (vs tanh_2 in franks-vals-fit). lambda_6 (TMD) is a runtime parameter (=0.016 in central); lambda_6_nu (CS) is hardcoded in scetlib (=0.0007).
important_conventions.no_internal_scale_10: LatticeNoConstraintsFranksLambda4Neg shares the franks branch: no helper kfactor=10.

chargeVgenNP0scetlibNPZlambda2.convention: Standard: Up = larger physical value (asymmetric: d_up=0.6, d_dn=0.4)

chargeVgenNP0scetlibNPZdelta_lambda2.convention: Standard: Up = larger physical value. NOMINAL = 0.

chargeVgenNP0scetlibNPZlambda4.convention: Standard: Up = larger physical value. Asymmetric reach into negative: d_up=0.6, d_dn=0.8.

TMD_lambda_6.note: Runtime parameter introduced in NP_models.hpp patch (was hardcoded 0.016 before). The corr also contains scan templates at 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5 but they are not used by this fit's NP model.

TMD_lambda_inf.note: Fixed in the fit; corr contains a 2.0 excursion template that is not used by this fit's NP model.

CS_lambda_6_nu.note: Hardcoded constant in scetlib (not a runtime parameter).
